zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester於1222年8月4日出生在英國素福爾可克萊爾時，他的父親Richard de Clare10歲，母親Isabel Marshal21歲。他於1238年2月2日與Maud de Lacy結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1262年7月14日在英國肯特沃爾坦享年39歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾福可斯波利福可斯波利 艾比。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: Claire
meanings: Irish: variant of   Clare  .
Variant of   Clair  , an altered form of German   Klare  .
French: variant of   Clair  .

variants.name: Chiara
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Marco, Amedeo, Angelo, Gino, Rocco.

Italian: from the female personal name Chiara or Clara, a name popularized in the medieval period by the cult of Saint Clare of Assisi (c. 1193–1253); she was an associate of Saint Francis of Assisi and founded the order of nuns known as the Poor Clares. See also Chiaro .

variants.name: Claar
meanings: German: variant of Klaar (and, in North America, an altered form of this), a nickname from Middle High German, Middle Low German klar ‘pure, pretty, sensible’, or a habitational name from a minor place so named near Braunschweig.
Possibly also an Americanized form of Dutch Klaar: from a vernacular form of the female personal name Clara (see Clare 2).

variants.name: Clarkin
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): from a pet form of the personal name Clare (compare Clare and Clear ), which is both a masculine and a female personal name in Middle English.
Irish (Monaghan): variant of   Clerkin  .

variants.name: Clear
meanings: English (southeastern England, of Norman origin): from Clere, the French popular form of the personal name Clare 2, and possibly interchangeable with it or confused with it.

English (southeastern): probably a habitational name from clere, a component of several placenames in north Hampshire (Highclere, Burghclere, Kingsclere). This is probably from a Celtic stream name meaning ‘bright’ (cognate with Latin clarus ‘clear, bright’).
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Klar   1.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester was born on 4 August 1222, in Clare, Suffolk, England, his father, Richard de Clare, was 10 and his mother, Isabel Marshal, was 21. He married Maud de Lacy on 2 February 1238. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. He died on 14 July 1262, in Waltham, Kent, England, at the age of 39, and was buried in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester est né le 4 août 1222, à Clare, Suffolk, Angleterre, son père, Richard de Clare, avait 10 ans et sa mère, Isabel Marshal, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Maud de Lacy le 2 février 1238. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 14 juillet 1262, à Waltham, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 39 ans, et a été enterré à Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester am 4. August 1222 in Clare, Suffolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Richard de Clare 10 und seine Mutter Isabel Marshal 21. Er hat am 2. Februar 1238 Maud de Lacy geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 14. Juli 1262 im Alter von 39 Jahren in Waltham, Kent, England und wurde in Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester è nato il il 4 agosto 1222 nel Clare, Suffolk, England, il suo padre, Richard de Clare, aveva 10 anni e il suo madre, Isabel Marshal, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Maud de Lacy il 2 febbraio 1238. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 14 luglio 1262, nel Waltham, Kent, England, all'età di 39 anni, e fu sepolto a Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester(이)가 1222년 8월 4일 영국 수폴크 클래르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Richard de Clare(은)는 10세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabel Marshal(은)는 21세였다. 그는 1238년 2월 2일 Maud de Lacy(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1262년 7월 14일 영국 켄트 월섬에서 39세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 튜케스버이 튜케스버이 애브브이에 묻혔다.

pt.Tewkesbury Abbey, Gloucestershire, Engla: Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.Tewkesbury Abbey, Gloucestershire, , Eng: Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester nasceu em 4 de agosto de 1222, em Clare, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard de Clare, tinha 10 anos e sua mãe, Isabel Marshal, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud de Lacy em 2 de fevereiro de 1238. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de julho de 1262, em Waltham, Kent, Inglaterra, com 39 anos, e foi sepultado em Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester родился 4 августа 1222 г. в местности клар, суффолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Richard de Clare было 10 лет, а матери, которую звали Isabel Marshal, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud de Lacy 2 февраля 1238 г. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Он умер 14 июля 1262 г. в местности валтам, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 39 лет и был похоронен в местности тюкесбури эбби, тюкесбури, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard de Clare 6th Earl of Gloucester nació el 4 de agosto de 1222, en Clare, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Richard de Clare, tenía 10 años y su mamá, Isabel Marshal, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Maud de Lacy el 2 de febrero de 1238. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 14 de julio de 1262, en Waltham, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 39 años, y fue sepultado en Tewkesbury Abbey, Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
